entries.description: Stage-1 exfil + stage-2 payload delivery endpoint observed in an April 2026 Contagious Interview campaign instance. Referenced from routes/api/auth.js as a base64-encoded URL. The endpoint accepts a POST with process.env as the body and responds with JavaScript that is executed via new Function(). Vercel free-subdomain infrastructure — attacker-controlled, no connection to any legitimate Vercel-hosted project.
C2 domain used by the axios RAT dropper (plain-crypto-js). All workstations and build servers should block this domain.
C2 IP used by the axios RAT.
C2 domain used by the ua-parser-js cryptominer/stealer payload (jsextension binary download).
Exfiltration endpoint used by the September 2024 chalk+debug typosquat wave. Pipedream webhook used to collect stolen environment variables.

pattern_iocs.description: Pattern of throwaway Vercel subdomains ending in '-six' used by multiple commodity crypto-stealer campaigns in 2026. Seen: ipcheck-six, dat3-six, miscommunication-six.
pattern_iocs.source: Cloudflare Cloudforce One, ANY.RUN sandbox reports

pattern_iocs.description: Pipedream webhook endpoints frequently abused as exfiltration receivers by npm supply chain attackers. Legitimate use exists but seeing a pipedream URL in source code of a dependency is suspicious.

notes: Treat all *.vercel.app, *.netlify.app, *.glitch.me, *.repl.co, *.ngrok.io subdomains with additional scrutiny — these are commonly abused because attackers can spin them up for free.
Add domains from CISA AA24 DPRK IT worker advisories, Treasury OFAC SDN list, Mandiant UNC5267 reports, any FBI IC3 PSAs on fake IT workers.
Priority additions for future updates: domains from Phylum PhantomRaven research, JFrog malicious package reports, Checkmarx supply chain writeups.
